implementation_summary: Final story of the direct-contact-profile-exchange epic; no production source changes. Added (1) a Playwright test hook seedDueProfileSchedule (app/tests/e2e/helpers/idb-record.ts, AC-E2E-1/FIXTURE-08-001) writing a due-now ProfileSchedule record directly into the few-dm-profile-schedule idb-keyval store, deliberately NOT a NEXT_PUBLIC_* build-time override; (2) a new epic-scoped e2e helper module (app/tests/e2e/helpers/dm-profile.ts) with contactCache/contacts-list read/seed/poll/assert helpers; (3) three new relay-bucket specs — dm-profile-self-heal.spec.ts (AC-PROF-7: pairs A/B for real, then clears each side's own contactCache entry for the other and drives convergence via seedDueProfileSchedule + reload alone, defeating the confound that a real pairing already completes both profiles via story S06's announce-on-pair before the pull loop runs), dm-profile-stranger-gates.spec.ts (AC-PROF-3/4: seeds ONLY the stranger's own lp_contacts_v1 belief so the stranger's real app constructs and publishes a genuine wire event, asserting the target's own records show no disclosure/injection/schedule-start), and dm-profile-push-triggers.spec.ts (AC-PROF-11b: pairing-instant profile both ways plus edit propagation, both via the real card/profile UI, no seedDueProfileSchedule at all); (4) two unit-level foreign-kind proof tests each in app/tests/unit/contactChat.test.ts and app/tests/unit/directMessageNotifications.test.ts (extending the existing kind-exclusion proof-test pattern), for both DM_PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND and DM_PROFILE_ANNOUNCE_KIND, with zero source change to ContactChat.tsx/directMessageNotifications.ts; (5) an i18n baseline confirmation (AC-PROF-14) via git diff of every S01-S07 source file plus a grep of all new dmProfile/*.ts files and ProfileHealWatcher.tsx, finding zero new user-facing strings and zero touch to i18n.ts; (6) the e2e tally bump in CLAUDE.md and Makefile (44->48 relay, 56->60 total, +4 new relay-bucket tests across the 3 new specs). REMEDIATION ROUND 1 (Stage-1, sev 4): the contactChat.test.ts AC-PROF-9 proof was replaced end-to-end — the original `mirrorsLiveGiftWrapDispatchGate` shadow function (asserted against its own re-implemented copy, no real production call, no observable side effect) was swapped for two cases reusing the file's existing real-driving `runInitBody` harness (the same pattern as the pre-existing 'gift wrap with non-matching peer pubkey is silently dropped' sibling test): a real gift-wrapped rumor of each new kind flows through the real parseDirectPayload/appendMessage/loadMessages pipeline, with the assertion reading back real persisted IDB state and the real upsertMessages call history for the specific rumor id.

verification_notes.unit_tests_run: cd app && npx vitest run tests/unit/contactChat.test.ts tests/unit/directMessageNotifications.test.ts -> both files green (14 and 17 tests respectively, including the 2+2 new proof tests, post-remediation). Full suite: npx vitest run -> 154/156 files, 2964/2989 tests passed; the 25 failing tests are the pre-existing, unrelated ThemeIcon/treatments icon-set baseline (see baseline.json), unchanged by this story.
verification_notes.remediation_round_1: Stage-1 review (sev 4, feedback_test_mocking_blind_spots anti-pattern) found the original AC-PROF-9 contactChat.test.ts proof was a shadow test: `mirrorsLiveGiftWrapDispatchGate` re-implemented ContactChat.tsx's dispatch order and asserted against its own copy, never calling real production code or observing a real side effect — it would not have failed had ContactChat.tsx gained a bubble path for the two new kinds. Fixed by replacing the whole describe block with two cases built on the file's existing real-driving `runInitBody` harness (real parseDirectPayload/appendMessage/loadMessages calls, real IDB read-back). While remediating, running the two new cases inside the full file (rather than filtered in isolation) surfaced pre-existing cross-describe state pollution — this file's module-level idbStore/upsertMessagesCalls are shared across every describe block, and an earlier describe's async self-heal/append-queue write was still landing after this describe's own beforeEach reset state — fixed by asserting containment of the specific rumor id (`upsertMessagesCalls.flat().some(...)`) rather than raw array length, which is robust to that pre-existing pollution without weakening what the test proves. Re-run: 14/14 (contactChat.test.ts alone), 31/31 (both proof files together), full suite unchanged at 2964/2989 (same 25 pre-existing unrelated failures).
verification_notes.e2e_relay_specs: The 3 new relay-bucket specs (dm-profile-self-heal.spec.ts, dm-profile-stranger-gates.spec.ts, dm-profile-push-triggers.spec.ts) require Docker (strfry:7777 + blossom:3001), unavailable in this implementation environment. Not executed here. Verified instead via: (a) a scoped ad hoc `tsc --noEmit` pass against tests/e2e/**/*.ts (tsconfig.json's own "exclude" list excludes the whole tests/ tree, a pre-existing project convention — NO_TYPECHECK for this tree by design) confirming the delta introduced by the 4 new files is exactly 4 instances of the SAME already-tolerated bootIdentity(browser, USER_B, ...) literal-type mismatch present in every other pre-existing spec that calls bootIdentity with USER_B/USER_C (e.g. dm-pairing-single-scan-mutual.spec.ts, contact-card-deeplink.spec.ts) — zero new error classes; (b) a manual read-through against the real app's testids (contact-card-*, profile-display-name, profile-avatar-thumb, profile-nickname-input, contact-added-success) and store shapes (few-dm-profile-schedule's ProfileSchedule, lp_contactCache_v1, lp_contacts_v1) confirmed via direct source reads (scheduler.ts, contactCache.ts, contacts.ts, ProfileHealWatcher.tsx, send.ts, contacts.tsx, ProfileSummary.tsx). The Step 5.8 e2e gate (lead-owned, real Docker) is the first actual execution of these 3 specs.
verification_notes.i18n_baseline: AC-PROF-14 confirmed clean: `git diff` over every S01-S07-modified source file (profile.tsx, Layout.tsx, contactCache.ts, pairingAck.ts, welcomeSubscription.ts) shows zero added JSX text/Alert/Heading/useToast/toast(/placeholder=/aria-label= lines; a grep of every new pure module (kinds.ts, scheduler.ts, send.ts, receive.ts) and ProfileHealWatcher.tsx (renders null) for the same patterns is empty; app/src/lib/i18n.ts is untouched (git status confirms no pending change). No action needed.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The story brief asked for e2e coverage of AC-PROF-7 (self-heal loop) and AC-PROF-3/4 (stranger gates), but neither scenario is directly producible by simply driving the UI once: a real pairing already completes both profiles via story S06's announce-on-pair before the pull loop ever gets a chance to run, and send.ts#sendProfileRequest/sendProfileAnnounce have no UI trigger for sending to an arbitrary, non-consenting pubkey (both are only ever invoked internally, over the SENDER's own contact list). Working out that clearing the SENDER's own local state after a real pairing (contactCache, for the self-heal anchor) and seeding ONLY the sender's own local contact belief (lp_contacts_v1, for the stranger gates) is the correct way to force each scenario for real — without touching the target's records or forging any wire event — required tracing every call site of sendProfileRequest/sendProfileAnnounce and reading contactCache.ts's LWW/read-back semantics closely before a single line of spec code could be written.
retrospective.surprised_by: app/pages/contacts.tsx's ContactListView has no reactivity wired to a contactCache.ts write (its contactsRevision counter only bumps on a group-membership change) — an already-mounted /contacts page will not show a live-arriving profile-announce without a fresh navigation/reload. The self-heal and push-trigger specs' convergence checks had to poll the raw localStorage state directly (mirroring helpers/pairing.ts#waitForAdmission's existing convention) rather than trust a rendered-DOM poll on an already-mounted page.

absorbed_from.note: Stage-1 sev-4: contactChat.test.ts's original AC-PROF-9 proof (mirrorsLiveGiftWrapDispatchGate) was a shadow test — reimplemented the dispatch order and asserted against its own copy with zero real production call and zero observed side effect, so it would not fail if ContactChat.tsx ever gained a bubble path for the two new kinds. Remediated by rebuilding the proof on the file's existing real-driving runInitBody harness (real parseDirectPayload/appendMessage/loadMessages, real IDB read-back), matching the fidelity of its 'gift wrap with non-matching peer pubkey' sibling test.

lead_disposition.examiner_partial: VQ-S08-012 PARTIAL is runtime validation of the relay e2e specs deferred to the Step 5.8 e2e gate (needs Docker) — the designed handoff, not a defect. Unit proofs 31/31 green; all ACs YES.
